source.name: Orchestra Research AI-Research-SKILLs
source.attribution: Catalog metadata derived from Orchestra Research upstream repository. Skill content remains credited to Orchestra Research and upstream project authors.

skills.name: Autoresearch
skills.description: Orchestrates end-to-end autonomous AI research projects using a two-loop architecture. The inner loop runs rapid experiment iterations with clear optimization targets. The outer loop synthesizes results, identifies patterns, and steers research direction. Routes to domain-specific skills for execution, supports continuous agent operation via Claude Code /loop and OpenClaw heartbeat, and produces research presentations and papers. Use when starting a research project, running autonomous experiments, or managing a multi-hypothesis research effort.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: LitGPT - Clean LLM Implementations
skills.description: Implements and trains LLMs using Lightning AI's LitGPT with 20+ pretrained architectures (Llama, Gemma, Phi, Qwen, Mistral). Use when need clean model implementations, educational understanding of architectures, or production fine-tuning with LoRA/QLoRA. Single-file implementations, no abstraction layers.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Mamba - Selective State Space Models
skills.description: State-space model with O(n) complexity vs Transformers' O(n²). 5× faster inference, million-token sequences, no KV cache. Selective SSM with hardware-aware design. Mamba-1 (d_state=16) and Mamba-2 (d_state=128, multi-head). Models 130M-2.8B on HuggingFace.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: nanoGPT - Minimalist GPT Training
skills.description: Educational GPT implementation in ~300 lines. Reproduces GPT-2 (124M) on OpenWebText. Clean, hackable code for learning transformers. By Andrej Karpathy. Perfect for understanding GPT architecture from scratch. Train on Shakespeare (CPU) or OpenWebText (multi-GPU).
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: RWKV - Receptance Weighted Key Value
skills.description: RNN+Transformer hybrid with O(n) inference. Linear time, infinite context, no KV cache. Train like GPT (parallel), infer like RNN (sequential). Linux Foundation AI project. Production at Windows, Office, NeMo. RWKV-7 (March 2025). Models up to 14B parameters.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: TorchTitan - PyTorch Native Distributed LLM Pretraining
skills.description: Provides PyTorch-native distributed LLM pretraining using torchtitan with 4D parallelism (FSDP2, TP, PP, CP). Use when pretraining Llama 3.1, DeepSeek V3, or custom models at scale from 8 to 512+ GPUs with Float8, torch.compile, and distributed checkpointing.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: HuggingFace Tokenizers - Fast Tokenization for NLP
skills.description: Fast tokenizers optimized for research and production. Rust-based implementation tokenizes 1GB in <20 seconds. Supports BPE, WordPiece, and Unigram algorithms. Train custom vocabularies, track alignments, handle padding/truncation. Integrates seamlessly with transformers. Use when you need high-performance tokenization or custom tokenizer training.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: SentencePiece - Language-Independent Tokenization
skills.description: Language-independent tokenizer treating text as raw Unicode. Supports BPE and Unigram algorithms. Fast (50k sentences/sec), lightweight (6MB memory), deterministic vocabulary. Used by T5, ALBERT, XLNet, mBART. Train on raw text without pre-tokenization. Use when you need multilingual support, CJK languages, or reproducible tokenization.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Axolotl Skill
skills.description: Expert guidance for fine-tuning LLMs with Axolotl - YAML configs, 100+ models, LoRA/QLoRA, DPO/KTO/ORPO/GRPO, multimodal support
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Llama-Factory Skill
skills.description: Expert guidance for fine-tuning LLMs with LLaMA-Factory - WebUI no-code, 100+ models, 2/3/4/5/6/8-bit QLoRA, multimodal support
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning)
skills.description: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning for LLMs using LoRA, QLoRA, and 25+ methods. Use when fine-tuning large models (7B-70B) with limited GPU memory, when you need to train <1% of parameters with minimal accuracy loss, or for multi-adapter serving. HuggingFace's official library integrated with transformers ecosystem.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Unsloth Skill
skills.description: Expert guidance for fast fine-tuning with Unsloth - 2-5x faster training, 50-80% less memory, LoRA/QLoRA optimization
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: nnsight: Transparent Access to Neural Network Internals
skills.description: Provides guidance for interpreting and manipulating neural network internals using nnsight with optional NDIF remote execution. Use when needing to run interpretability experiments on massive models (70B+) without local GPU resources, or when working with any PyTorch architecture.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: pyvene: Causal Interventions for Neural Networks
skills.description: Provides guidance for performing causal interventions on PyTorch models using pyvene's declarative intervention framework. Use when conducting causal tracing, activation patching, interchange intervention training, or testing causal hypotheses about model behavior.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: SAELens: Sparse Autoencoders for Mechanistic Interpretability
skills.description: Provides guidance for training and analyzing Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) using SAELens to decompose neural network activations into interpretable features. Use when discovering interpretable features, analyzing superposition, or studying monosemantic representations in language models.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: TransformerLens: Mechanistic Interpretability for Transformers
skills.description: Provides guidance for mechanistic interpretability research using TransformerLens to inspect and manipulate transformer internals via HookPoints and activation caching. Use when reverse-engineering model algorithms, studying attention patterns, or performing activation patching experiments.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: NeMo Curator - GPU-Accelerated Data Curation
skills.description: GPU-accelerated data curation for LLM training. Supports text/image/video/audio. Features fuzzy deduplication (16× faster), quality filtering (30+ heuristics), semantic deduplication, PII redaction, NSFW detection. Scales across GPUs with RAPIDS. Use for preparing high-quality training datasets, cleaning web data, or deduplicating large corpora.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Ray Data - Scalable ML Data Processing
skills.description: Scalable data processing for ML workloads. Streaming execution across CPU/GPU, supports Parquet/CSV/JSON/images. Integrates with Ray Train, PyTorch, TensorFlow. Scales from single machine to 100s of nodes. Use for batch inference, data preprocessing, multi-modal data loading, or distributed ETL pipelines.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: GRPO/RL Training with TRL
skills.description: Expert guidance for GRPO/RL fine-tuning with TRL for reasoning and task-specific model training
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: miles: Enterprise-Grade RL for Large-Scale Model Training
skills.description: Provides guidance for enterprise-grade RL training using miles, a production-ready fork of slime. Use when training large MoE models with FP8/INT4, needing train-inference alignment, or requiring speculative RL for maximum throughput.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: OpenRLHF - High-Performance RLHF Training
skills.description: High-performance RLHF framework with Ray+vLLM acceleration. Use for PPO, GRPO, RLOO, DPO training of large models (7B-70B+). Built on Ray, vLLM, ZeRO-3. 2× faster than DeepSpeedChat with distributed architecture and GPU resource sharing.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: SimPO - Simple Preference Optimization
skills.description: Simple Preference Optimization for LLM alignment. Reference-free alternative to DPO with better performance (+6.4 points on AlpacaEval 2.0). No reference model needed, more efficient than DPO. Use for preference alignment when want simpler, faster training than DPO/PPO.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: slime: LLM Post-Training Framework for RL Scaling
skills.description: Provides guidance for LLM post-training with RL using slime, a Megatron+SGLang framework. Use when training GLM models, implementing custom data generation workflows, or needing tight Megatron-LM integration for RL scaling.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: torchforge: PyTorch-Native Agentic RL Library
skills.description: Provides guidance for PyTorch-native agentic RL using torchforge, Meta's library separating infra from algorithms. Use when you want clean RL abstractions, easy algorithm experimentation, or scalable training with Monarch and TorchTitan.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: TRL - Transformer Reinforcement Learning
skills.description: Fine-tune LLMs using reinforcement learning with TRL - SFT for instruction tuning, DPO for preference alignment, PPO/GRPO for reward optimization, and reward model training. Use when need RLHF, align model with preferences, or train from human feedback. Works with HuggingFace Transformers.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: verl: Volcano Engine Reinforcement Learning for LLMs
skills.description: Provides guidance for training LLMs with reinforcement learning using verl (Volcano Engine RL). Use when implementing RLHF, GRPO, PPO, or other RL algorithms for LLM post-training at scale with flexible infrastructure backends.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Constitutional AI - Harmlessness from AI Feedback
skills.description: Anthropic's method for training harmless AI through self-improvement. Two-phase approach - supervised learning with self-critique/revision, then RLAIF (RL from AI Feedback). Use for safety alignment, reducing harmful outputs without human labels. Powers Claude's safety system.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: LlamaGuard - AI Content Moderation
skills.description: Meta's 7-8B specialized moderation model for LLM input/output filtering. 6 safety categories - violence/hate, sexual content, weapons, substances, self-harm, criminal planning. 94-95% accuracy. Deploy with vLLM, HuggingFace, Sagemaker. Integrates with NeMo Guardrails.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: NeMo Guardrails - Programmable Safety for LLMs
skills.description: NVIDIA's runtime safety framework for LLM applications. Features jailbreak detection, input/output validation, fact-checking, hallucination detection, PII filtering, toxicity detection. Uses Colang 2.0 DSL for programmable rails. Production-ready, runs on T4 GPU.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Prompt Guard - Prompt Injection & Jailbreak Detection
skills.description: Meta's 86M prompt injection and jailbreak detector. Filters malicious prompts and third-party data for LLM apps. 99%+ TPR, <1% FPR. Fast (<2ms GPU). Multilingual (8 languages). Deploy with HuggingFace or batch processing for RAG security.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: HuggingFace Accelerate - Unified Distributed Training
skills.description: Simplest distributed training API. 4 lines to add distributed support to any PyTorch script. Unified API for DeepSpeed/FSDP/Megatron/DDP. Automatic device placement, mixed precision (FP16/BF16/FP8). Interactive config, single launch command. HuggingFace ecosystem standard.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Deepspeed Skill
skills.description: Expert guidance for distributed training with DeepSpeed - ZeRO optimization stages, pipeline parallelism, FP16/BF16/FP8, 1-bit Adam, sparse attention
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Megatron-Core - Large-Scale LLM Training
skills.description: Trains large language models (2B-462B parameters) using NVIDIA Megatron-Core with advanced parallelism strategies. Use when training models >1B parameters, need maximum GPU efficiency (47% MFU on H100), or require tensor/pipeline/sequence/context/expert parallelism. Production-ready framework used for Nemotron, LLaMA, DeepSeek.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Skill: Use PyTorch FSDP2 (`fully_shard`) correctly in a training script
skills.description: Adds PyTorch FSDP2 (fully_shard) to training scripts with correct init, sharding, mixed precision/offload config, and distributed checkpointing. Use when models exceed single-GPU memory or when you need DTensor-based sharding with DeviceMesh.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: PyTorch Lightning - High-Level Training Framework
skills.description: High-level PyTorch framework with Trainer class, automatic distributed training (DDP/FSDP/DeepSpeed), callbacks system, and minimal boilerplate. Scales from laptop to supercomputer with same code. Use when you want clean training loops with built-in best practices.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Ray Train - Distributed Training Orchestration
skills.description: Distributed training orchestration across clusters. Scales PyTorch/TensorFlow/HuggingFace from laptop to 1000s of nodes. Built-in hyperparameter tuning with Ray Tune, fault tolerance, elastic scaling. Use when training massive models across multiple machines or running distributed hyperparameter sweeps.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Lambda Labs GPU Cloud
skills.description: Reserved and on-demand GPU cloud instances for ML training and inference. Use when you need dedicated GPU instances with simple SSH access, persistent filesystems, or high-performance multi-node clusters for large-scale training.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Modal Serverless GPU
skills.description: Serverless GPU cloud platform for running ML workloads. Use when you need on-demand GPU access without infrastructure management, deploying ML models as APIs, or running batch jobs with automatic scaling.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: SkyPilot Multi-Cloud Orchestration
skills.description: Multi-cloud orchestration for ML workloads with automatic cost optimization. Use when you need to run training or batch jobs across multiple clouds, leverage spot instances with auto-recovery, or optimize GPU costs across providers.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: AWQ (Activation-aware Weight Quantization)
skills.description: Activation-aware weight quantization for 4-bit LLM compression with 3x speedup and minimal accuracy loss. Use when deploying large models (7B-70B) on limited GPU memory, when you need faster inference than GPTQ with better accuracy preservation, or for instruction-tuned and multimodal models. MLSys 2024 Best Paper Award winner.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: bitsandbytes - LLM Quantization
skills.description: Quantizes LLMs to 8-bit or 4-bit for 50-75% memory reduction with minimal accuracy loss. Use when GPU memory is limited, need to fit larger models, or want faster inference. Supports INT8, NF4, FP4 formats, QLoRA training, and 8-bit optimizers. Works with HuggingFace Transformers.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Flash Attention - Fast Memory-Efficient Attention
skills.description: Optimizes transformer attention with Flash Attention for 2-4x speedup and 10-20x memory reduction. Use when training/running transformers with long sequences (>512 tokens), encountering GPU memory issues with attention, or need faster inference. Supports PyTorch native SDPA, flash-attn library, H100 FP8, and sliding window attention.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: GGUF - Quantization Format for llama.cpp
skills.description: GGUF format and llama.cpp quantization for efficient CPU/GPU inference. Use when deploying models on consumer hardware, Apple Silicon, or when needing flexible quantization from 2-8 bit without GPU requirements.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: GPTQ (Generative Pre-trained Transformer Quantization)
skills.description: Post-training 4-bit quantization for LLMs with minimal accuracy loss. Use for deploying large models (70B, 405B) on consumer GPUs, when you need 4× memory reduction with <2% perplexity degradation, or for faster inference (3-4× speedup) vs FP16. Integrates with transformers and PEFT for QLoRA fine-tuning.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: HQQ - Half-Quadratic Quantization
skills.description: Half-Quadratic Quantization for LLMs without calibration data. Use when quantizing models to 4/3/2-bit precision without needing calibration datasets, for fast quantization workflows, or when deploying with vLLM or HuggingFace Transformers.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: ML Training Recipes
skills.description: Battle-tested PyTorch training recipes for all domains — LLMs, vision, diffusion, medical imaging, protein/drug discovery, spatial omics, genomics. Covers training loops, optimizer selection (AdamW, Muon), LR scheduling, mixed precision, debugging, and systematic experimentation. Use when training or fine-tuning neural networks, debugging loss spikes or OOM, choosing architectures, or optimizing GPU throughput.
skills.author: dailycafi

skills.name: BigCode Evaluation Harness - Code Model Benchmarking
skills.description: Evaluates code generation models across HumanEval, MBPP, MultiPL-E, and 15+ benchmarks with pass@k metrics. Use when benchmarking code models, comparing coding abilities, testing multi-language support, or measuring code generation quality. Industry standard from BigCode Project used by HuggingFace leaderboards.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: lm-evaluation-harness - LLM Benchmarking
skills.description: Evaluates LLMs across 60+ academic benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K, TruthfulQA, HellaSwag). Use when benchmarking model quality, comparing models, reporting academic results, or tracking training progress. Industry standard used by EleutherAI, HuggingFace, and major labs. Supports HuggingFace, vLLM, APIs.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: NeMo Evaluator SDK - Enterprise LLM Benchmarking
skills.description: Evaluates LLMs across 100+ benchmarks from 18+ harnesses (MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K, safety, VLM) with multi-backend execution. Use when needing scalable evaluation on local Docker, Slurm HPC, or cloud platforms. NVIDIA's enterprise-grade platform with container-first architecture for reproducible benchmarking.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: llama.cpp
skills.description: Runs LLM inference on CPU, Apple Silicon, and consumer GPUs without NVIDIA hardware. Use for edge deployment, M1/M2/M3 Macs, AMD/Intel GPUs, or when CUDA is unavailable. Supports GGUF quantization (1.5-8 bit) for reduced memory and 4-10× speedup vs PyTorch on CPU.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: SGLang
skills.description: Fast structured generation and serving for LLMs with RadixAttention prefix caching. Use for JSON/regex outputs, constrained decoding, agentic workflows with tool calls, or when you need 5× faster inference than vLLM with prefix sharing. Powers 300,000+ GPUs at xAI, AMD, NVIDIA, and LinkedIn.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: TensorRT-LLM
skills.description: Optimizes LLM inference with NVIDIA TensorRT for maximum throughput and lowest latency. Use for production deployment on NVIDIA GPUs (A100/H100), when you need 10-100x faster inference than PyTorch, or for serving models with quantization (FP8/INT4), in-flight batching, and multi-GPU scaling.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: vLLM - High-Performance LLM Serving
skills.description: Serves LLMs with high throughput using vLLM's PagedAttention and continuous batching. Use when deploying production LLM APIs, optimizing inference latency/throughput, or serving models with limited GPU memory. Supports OpenAI-compatible endpoints, quantization (GPTQ/AWQ/FP8), and tensor parallelism.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: MLflow: ML Lifecycle Management Platform
skills.description: Track ML experiments, manage model registry with versioning, deploy models to production, and reproduce experiments with MLflow - framework-agnostic ML lifecycle platform
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: SwanLab: Open-Source Experiment Tracking
skills.description: Provides guidance for experiment tracking with SwanLab. Use when you need open-source run tracking, local or self-hosted dashboards, and lightweight media logging for ML workflows.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: TensorBoard: Visualization Toolkit for ML
skills.description: Visualize training metrics, debug models with histograms, compare experiments, visualize model graphs, and profile performance with TensorBoard - Google's ML visualization toolkit
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Weights & Biases: ML Experiment Tracking & MLOps
skills.description: Track ML experiments with automatic logging, visualize training in real-time, optimize hyperparameters with sweeps, and manage model registry with W&B - collaborative MLOps platform
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Evolving AI Agents with A-Evolve
skills.description: Provides guidance for automatically evolving and optimizing AI agents across any domain using LLM-driven evolution algorithms. Use when building self-improving agents, optimizing agent prompts and skills against benchmarks, or implementing automated agent evaluation loops.
skills.author: A-EVO Lab

skills.name: AutoGPT - Autonomous AI Agent Platform
skills.description: Autonomous AI agent platform for building and deploying continuous agents. Use when creating visual workflow agents, deploying persistent autonomous agents, or building complex multi-step AI automation systems.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: CrewAI - Multi-Agent Orchestration Framework
skills.description: Multi-agent orchestration framework for autonomous AI collaboration. Use when building teams of specialized agents working together on complex tasks, when you need role-based agent collaboration with memory, or for production workflows requiring sequential/hierarchical execution. Built without LangChain dependencies for lean, fast execution.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: LangChain - Build LLM Applications with Agents & RAG
skills.description: Framework for building LLM-powered applications with agents, chains, and RAG. Supports multiple providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google), 500+ integrations, ReAct agents, tool calling, memory management, and vector store retrieval. Use for building chatbots, question-answering systems, autonomous agents, or RAG applications. Best for rapid prototyping and production deployments.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: LlamaIndex - Data Framework for LLM Applications
skills.description: Data framework for building LLM applications with RAG. Specializes in document ingestion (300+ connectors), indexing, and querying. Features vector indices, query engines, agents, and multi-modal support. Use for document Q&A, chatbots, knowledge retrieval, or building RAG pipelines. Best for data-centric LLM applications.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Chroma - Open-Source Embedding Database
skills.description: Open-source embedding database for AI applications. Store embeddings and metadata, perform vector and full-text search, filter by metadata. Simple 4-function API. Scales from notebooks to production clusters. Use for semantic search, RAG applications, or document retrieval. Best for local development and open-source projects.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: FAISS - Efficient Similarity Search
skills.description: Facebook's library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. Supports billions of vectors, GPU acceleration, and various index types (Flat, IVF, HNSW). Use for fast k-NN search, large-scale vector retrieval, or when you need pure similarity search without metadata. Best for high-performance applications.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Pinecone - Managed Vector Database
skills.description: Managed vector database for production AI applications. Fully managed, auto-scaling, with hybrid search (dense + sparse), metadata filtering, and namespaces. Low latency (<100ms p95). Use for production RAG, recommendation systems, or semantic search at scale. Best for serverless, managed infrastructure.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Qdrant - Vector Similarity Search Engine
skills.description: High-performance vector similarity search engine for RAG and semantic search. Use when building production RAG systems requiring fast nearest neighbor search, hybrid search with filtering, or scalable vector storage with Rust-powered performance.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Sentence Transformers - State-of-the-Art Embeddings
skills.description: Framework for state-of-the-art sentence, text, and image embeddings. Provides 5000+ pre-trained models for semantic similarity, clustering, and retrieval. Supports multilingual, domain-specific, and multimodal models. Use for generating embeddings for RAG, semantic search, or similarity tasks. Best for production embedding generation.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: DSPy: Declarative Language Model Programming
skills.description: Build complex AI systems with declarative programming, optimize prompts automatically, create modular RAG systems and agents with DSPy - Stanford NLP's framework for systematic LM programming
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Guidance: Constrained LLM Generation
skills.description: Control LLM output with regex and grammars, guarantee valid JSON/XML/code generation, enforce structured formats, and build multi-step workflows with Guidance - Microsoft Research's constrained generation framework
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Instructor: Structured LLM Outputs
skills.description: Extract structured data from LLM responses with Pydantic validation, retry failed extractions automatically, parse complex JSON with type safety, and stream partial results with Instructor - battle-tested structured output library
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Outlines: Structured Text Generation
skills.description: Guarantee valid JSON/XML/code structure during generation, use Pydantic models for type-safe outputs, support local models (Transformers, vLLM), and maximize inference speed with Outlines - dottxt.ai's structured generation library
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: LangSmith - LLM Observability Platform
skills.description: LLM observability platform for tracing, evaluation, and monitoring. Use when debugging LLM applications, evaluating model outputs against datasets, monitoring production systems, or building systematic testing pipelines for AI applications.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Phoenix - AI Observability Platform
skills.description: Open-source AI observability platform for LLM tracing, evaluation, and monitoring. Use when debugging LLM applications with detailed traces, running evaluations on datasets, or monitoring production AI systems with real-time insights.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: AudioCraft: Audio Generation
skills.description: PyTorch library for audio generation including text-to-music (MusicGen) and text-to-sound (AudioGen). Use when you need to generate music from text descriptions, create sound effects, or perform melody-conditioned music generation.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: BLIP-2: Vision-Language Pre-training
skills.description: Vision-language pre-training framework bridging frozen image encoders and LLMs. Use when you need image captioning, visual question answering, image-text retrieval, or multimodal chat with state-of-the-art zero-shot performance.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: CLIP - Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training
skills.description: OpenAI's model connecting vision and language. Enables zero-shot image classification, image-text matching, and cross-modal retrieval. Trained on 400M image-text pairs. Use for image search, content moderation, or vision-language tasks without fine-tuning. Best for general-purpose image understanding.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Cosmos Policy Evaluation
skills.description: Evaluates NVIDIA Cosmos Policy on LIBERO and RoboCasa simulation environments. Use when setting up cosmos-policy for robot manipulation evaluation, running headless GPU evaluations with EGL rendering, or profiling inference latency on cluster or local GPU machines.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: LLaVA - Large Language and Vision Assistant
skills.description: Large Language and Vision Assistant. Enables visual instruction tuning and image-based conversations. Combines CLIP vision encoder with Vicuna/LLaMA language models. Supports multi-turn image chat, visual question answering, and instruction following. Use for vision-language chatbots or image understanding tasks. Best for conversational image analysis.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: OpenPI Fine-Tuning and Serving
skills.description: Fine-tune and serve Physical Intelligence OpenPI models (pi0, pi0-fast, pi0.5) using JAX or PyTorch backends for robot policy inference across ALOHA, DROID, and LIBERO environments. Use when adapting pi0 models to custom datasets, converting JAX checkpoints to PyTorch, running policy inference servers, or debugging norm stats and GPU memory issues.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: OpenVLA-OFT
skills.description: Fine-tunes and evaluates OpenVLA-OFT and OpenVLA-OFT+ policies for robot action generation with continuous action heads, LoRA adaptation, and FiLM conditioning on LIBERO simulation and ALOHA real-world setups. Use when reproducing OpenVLA-OFT paper results, training custom VLA action heads (L1 or diffusion), deploying server-client inference for ALOHA, or debugging normalization, LoRA merge, and cross-GPU issues.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Segment Anything Model (SAM)
skills.description: Foundation model for image segmentation with zero-shot transfer. Use when you need to segment any object in images using points, boxes, or masks as prompts, or automatically generate all object masks in an image.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Stable Diffusion Image Generation
skills.description: State-of-the-art text-to-image generation with Stable Diffusion models via HuggingFace Diffusers. Use when generating images from text prompts, performing image-to-image translation, inpainting, or building custom diffusion pipelines.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Whisper - Robust Speech Recognition
skills.description: OpenAI's general-purpose speech recognition model. Supports 99 languages, transcription, translation to English, and language identification. Six model sizes from tiny (39M params) to large (1550M params). Use for speech-to-text, podcast transcription, or multilingual audio processing. Best for robust, multilingual ASR.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Knowledge Distillation: Compressing LLMs
skills.description: Compress large language models using knowledge distillation from teacher to student models. Use when deploying smaller models with retained performance, transferring GPT-4 capabilities to open-source models, or reducing inference costs. Covers temperature scaling, soft targets, reverse KLD, logit distillation, and MiniLLM training strategies.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Long Context: Extending Transformer Context Windows
skills.description: Extend context windows of transformer models using RoPE, YaRN, ALiBi, and position interpolation techniques. Use when processing long documents (32k-128k+ tokens), extending pre-trained models beyond original context limits, or implementing efficient positional encodings. Covers rotary embeddings, attention biases, interpolation methods, and extrapolation strategies for LLMs.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Model Merging: Combining Pre-trained Models
skills.description: Merge multiple fine-tuned models using mergekit to combine capabilities without retraining. Use when creating specialized models by blending domain-specific expertise (math + coding + chat), improving performance beyond single models, or experimenting rapidly with model variants. Covers SLERP, TIES-Merging, DARE, Task Arithmetic, linear merging, and production deployment strategies.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Model Pruning: Compressing LLMs
skills.description: Reduce LLM size and accelerate inference using pruning techniques like Wanda and SparseGPT. Use when compressing models without retraining, achieving 50% sparsity with minimal accuracy loss, or enabling faster inference on hardware accelerators. Covers unstructured pruning, structured pruning, N:M sparsity, magnitude pruning, and one-shot methods.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: MoE Training: Mixture of Experts
skills.description: Train Mixture of Experts (MoE) models using DeepSpeed or HuggingFace. Use when training large-scale models with limited compute (5× cost reduction vs dense models), implementing sparse architectures like Mixtral 8x7B or DeepSeek-V3, or scaling model capacity without proportional compute increase. Covers MoE architectures, routing mechanisms, load balancing, expert parallelism, and inference optimization.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Speculative Decoding: Accelerating LLM Inference
skills.description: Accelerate LLM inference using speculative decoding, Medusa multiple heads, and lookahead decoding techniques. Use when optimizing inference speed (1.5-3.6× speedup), reducing latency for real-time applications, or deploying models with limited compute. Covers draft models, tree-based attention, Jacobi iteration, parallel token generation, and production deployment strategies.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Academic Plotting for ML Papers
skills.description: Generates publication-quality figures for ML papers from research context. Given a paper section or description, extracts system components and relationships to generate architecture diagrams via Gemini. Given experiment results or data, auto-selects chart type and generates data-driven figures via matplotlib/seaborn. Use when creating any figure for a conference paper.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: ML Paper Writing for Top AI Conferences
skills.description: Write publication-ready ML/AI papers for NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ACL, AAAI, COLM. Use when drafting papers from research repos, structuring arguments, verifying citations, or preparing camera-ready submissions. For systems venues (OSDI, NSDI, ASPLOS, SOSP), use systems-paper-writing instead.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Presenting Conference Talks: From Paper to Slides
skills.description: Generates conference presentation slides (Beamer LaTeX PDF and editable PPTX) from a compiled paper with speaker notes and talk script. Use when preparing oral talks, spotlight presentations, or invited talks for ML and systems conferences.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Systems Paper Writing: Paragraph-Level Structural Blueprint
skills.description: Comprehensive guide for writing systems papers targeting OSDI, SOSP, ASPLOS, NSDI, and EuroSys. Provides paragraph-level structural blueprints, writing patterns, venue-specific checklists, reviewer guidelines, LaTeX templates, and conference deadlines. Use this skill for all systems conference paper writing.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Research Idea Brainstorming
skills.description: Guides researchers through structured ideation frameworks to discover high-impact research directions. Use when exploring new problem spaces, pivoting between projects, or seeking novel angles on existing work.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

skills.name: Creative Thinking for Research
skills.description: Applies cognitive science frameworks for creative thinking to CS and AI research ideation. Use when seeking genuinely novel research directions by leveraging combinatorial creativity, analogical reasoning, constraint manipulation, and other empirically grounded creative strategies.
skills.author: Orchestra Research

bundles.name: Research Foundation
bundles.summary: Autonomous research loop, ideation, literature, and paper-output essentials.
bundles.description: Start here when you want one MUTX operator to run end-to-end research cycles from question framing to write-up.

bundles.name: RAG & Retrieval Stack
bundles.summary: Retrieval, vector search, embeddings, and agent frameworks for repo-aware assistants.
bundles.description: Use when you want MUTX assistants to ingest code/docs, retrieve context, and answer with citations or grounded file references.

bundles.name: Post-Training Lab
bundles.summary: Fine-tuning and RLHF/GRPO stack for adapting models inside MUTX workflows.
bundles.description: Use for model adaptation, preference optimization, and reinforcement learning experiments tied to operator or product data.

bundles.name: Inference & Optimization
bundles.summary: Serving engines plus quantization and attention optimizations for production runtime lanes.
bundles.description: Use when you need a shipping inference stack, cost reduction, and low-latency runtime design inside MUTX.

bundles.name: Multimodal + Safety
bundles.summary: Vision/audio model stack with guardrails and observability.
bundles.description: Use when you need multimodal product work but still want moderation, tracing, and policy layers.

swarm_blueprints.name: Research Triad
swarm_blueprints.summary: One orchestrator, one literature scout, one experiment executor.
swarm_blueprints.description: Shipping default for research work inside MUTX. Split discovery, synthesis, and execution so the orchestrator is not drowning in implementation detail.

roles.title: Research Orchestrator
roles.goal: Own the question, findings.md equivalent, and next-step selection.

roles.title: Literature Scout
roles.goal: Search papers, collect benchmarks, and ground claims with sources.

roles.title: Experiment Executor
roles.goal: Run narrow experiments, benchmarks, and implementation spikes.

swarm_blueprints.coordination_notes: Use the orchestrator to assign bounded tasks, require artifact handoffs, and review after every loop.

swarm_blueprints.name: Trainer / Eval / Safety
swarm_blueprints.summary: Three-lane training stack for model adaptation work.
swarm_blueprints.description: Use when MUTX is running model adaptation or policy experiments and you want a hard separation between training, benchmarking, and safety review.

roles.title: Trainer
roles.goal: Own datasets, configs, checkpoints, and training loops.

roles.title: Evaluator
roles.goal: Benchmark checkpoints, regressions, and serving readiness.

roles.title: Safety Reviewer
roles.goal: Check moderation, jailbreak exposure, and telemetry coverage.

swarm_blueprints.coordination_notes: Promote checkpoints only when evaluator and safety reviewer both pass.

swarm_blueprints.name: RAG Ship Room
swarm_blueprints.summary: Retrieval architect, data/index lane, and product integrator.
swarm_blueprints.description: Use for repo-aware assistant launches and grounded operator copilots.

roles.title: Retrieval Architect
roles.goal: Design chunking, embedding, reranking, and answer composition.

roles.title: Index & Data Lane
roles.goal: Keep ingestion and vector stores healthy and reproducible.

roles.title: Product Integrator
roles.goal: Wire the retrieval layer into live MUTX routes and UX.

swarm_blueprints.coordination_notes: Treat retrieval quality and product latency as two separate gates.

swarm_blueprints.name: Inference Ops Lane
swarm_blueprints.summary: Serve, benchmark, and cost-optimize runtime lanes.
swarm_blueprints.description: Use for production inference architecture reviews and rollout planning.

roles.title: Serving Engineer
roles.goal: Own engine choice, serving topology, and runtime tuning.

roles.title: Benchmark Engineer
roles.goal: Measure latency, quality, and throughput under realistic MUTX loads.

roles.title: Cost Optimizer
roles.goal: Choose quantization and model shapes that hit budget constraints.

swarm_blueprints.coordination_notes: Never ship a serving change without benchmark diffs and rollback notes.

templates.name: Orchestra Research Foundation
templates.summary: Deploy a MUTX research operator with the core Orchestra research stack preloaded.
templates.description: Single-agent starter with autoresearch, ideation, retrieval, and paper-output skills wired into one workspace.

templates.name: Orchestra RAG Lab
templates.summary: Deploy a repo-aware retrieval assistant stack for grounded answers and document search.
templates.description: Starter for retrieval-heavy product work: embeddings, vector stores, and agent frameworks tuned for grounded operator assistants.

templates.name: Orchestra Post-Training Lab
templates.summary: Deploy a training operator for fine-tuning, GRPO, and evaluation work.
templates.description: Starter for adaptation work with the core fine-tuning and post-training stack already selected.

templates.name: Orchestra Inference Lab
templates.summary: Deploy a serving and optimization operator for production runtime work.
templates.description: Starter focused on vLLM, SGLang, quantization, and inference optimization for shipping runtime lanes.
